channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The anonymous social app that thinks it can work in Saudi Arabia
item.description: When Fizz quietly debuted in Saudi Arabia, founder and CEO Teddy Solomon wasn’t expecting the app to catch on like it did.

item.title: Forget the tower: Dell’s palm-sized desktop draws 100W through USB-C and still manages 50 TOPS of AI computing power
item.description: Dell’s Pro 5 Micro Desktop delivers AI acceleration, high-speed DDR5 memory, and USB-C powered compact design for tight office spaces.

item.title: Two Songs We Really Wish Were the Anime Outros for ‘Steel Ball Run’
item.description: If Netflix, Warner Bros. Japan, and David Production really want to appease disgruntled 'JoJo's Bizarre Adventure' fans, they should make either of these two bops the ending themes for 'Steel Ball Run.'

item.title: AI Training Data Giant Mercor Is Reportedly Looking to Buy the Work You Did at Your Old Job
Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

item.title: Scientists Have Made a French Fry Breakthrough
item.description: Researchers have developed a method of making french fries that results in a healthier bite without sacrificing crispiness.

item.title: Utah Is Giving Dr. AI the Power to Renew Drug Prescriptions
item.description: Dr. AI will see you now.

item.title: AI animation studio Toonstar will turn books into digital shows for HarperCollins
item.description: HarperCollins is tapping into AI to bring some of its book franchises to life. Specifically, the publisher is teaming up with Toonstar, an AI animation studio, to turn them into digital shows. The first project will be an adaptation of Lisa Greenwald’s “Friendship List” series, which will also be joined by a graphic novel. You’d be forgiven for being unaware of Toonstar, a studio that received some buzzy early on for simplifying typically complex animation pipelines with AI, but has mostly remained under the radar. Its biggest claim to fame is producing StEvEn and Parker YouTube series, which ...

item.title: Here’s the Perfect Excuse to See ‘Project Hail Mary’ Again
item.description: Directors Phil Lord and Chris Miller recorded a director commentary you can listen to in the theater.

item.title: NASA did eventually solve Artemis II’s Outlook glitch
item.description: On Thursday, during Artemis II's journey to the Moon, commander Reid Wiseman ran into a tech issue some of us back on Earth can relate to: Microsoft Outlook wasn't working. In a conversation captured in NASA's Artemis livestream and shared on Bluesky, Wiseman reported to Mission Control: "I also see that I have two Microsoft […]

item.title: Why people are suddenly hesitant to watch Zendaya’s new movie, “The Drama”
item.description: A key plot reveal and public criticism are changing how audiences see The Drama. When a fake wedding announcement featuring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson popped up in a late-year print issue The Boston Globe, it looked like a star-studded romcom was underway. But “The Drama,” indie studio A24’s latest film, has stirred controversy, and most audiences don’t know what its about....

item.title: Tesla’s Texas factory workforce reportedly shrunk 22% in 2025
item.description: Tesla's headcount fell from 21,191 workers to 16,506 workers in 2025, according to a report, as it grappled with its second straight year of declining sales.
